goal: Harden the repository's agent architecture with typed context packets and bounded context management.
constraints: Keep the rollout incremental and testable in isolation.
Use the existing orchestrator as the default path instead of introducing a new agent swarm.
Do not claim deterministic editor-side token enforcement that the repo cannot guarantee.

completed_steps.summary: Mapped the orchestrator, prompt wrappers, manifest, run-artifact schema, and validation tooling.
completed_steps.output: Top weaknesses identified: freeform context passing, no bounded task-local scratchpad policy, and no explicit context-health gate.
completed_steps.verification: Read the workflow manifest, orchestrator instructions, prompt wrappers, and sync validation script.

current_step.title: Introduce the canonical context packet and task telemetry contracts
current_step.input_contract: Current repo agent-platform metadata, active spec, and prompt files.
current_step.output_contract: Updated schemas, manifest policy, and validator support for the context packet plus task ledger.
current_step.done_condition: The new packet artifact, examples, and validator all align and the prompt layer can reference them.

task_graph.title: Add the canonical context packet and update the run-artifact telemetry contract
task_graph.input_contract: Agent-platform metadata, validator script, and example artifacts.
task_graph.output_contract: New schema, example packet, extended run-artifact schema, and passing validation.
task_graph.done_condition: Metadata and validation tooling agree on the new contract.

task_graph.title: Apply the context packet, task-router, and memory-tier policy to the default orchestration path
task_graph.output_contract: Structured goal anchor, atomic task decomposition, and context-health instructions in the default path.
task_graph.done_condition: The orchestrator and prompt wrappers describe the same per-step context model.

task_graph.title: Document the new context model and incremental rollout
task_graph.input_contract: Root guide, setup guide, and runbook.
task_graph.output_contract: Contributor docs that match the implemented metadata and prompt contract.
task_graph.done_condition: The runbook and setup guide describe the same context-engineering model as the manifest.

working_memory.focus: Agent-platform metadata and validator changes for phase 1.
scratchpad: Extend run-artifact schema with taskLedger telemetry.
Update validator and prompt wrappers to reference the new contract.
working_memory.stale_data_policy: Replace the scratchpad between subtasks and do not retain raw tool output after summarizing it into episodic memory.

episodic_memory.episode_summary: Architecture scan completed. The default orchestrator path will be hardened first; specialist-agent expansion is deferred.
retained_facts: workflow-manifest.json is the canonical routing metadata layer.
Prompt wrappers still use freeform trailing context fields today.
sync_agent_platform.py already validates run-artifact examples and can be extended for the new packet.

semantic_memory.retrieval_policy: Load repo facts on demand only when the active subtask cannot proceed from working or episodic memory.

loaded_memories.summary: Agent customization changes should prefer existing workspace instructions, prompts, agents, and skills instead of inventing a new primitive.

run_artifact.task_ledger_policy: Append a taskLedger entry after each completed subtask when the workflow spans multiple surfaces or evidence sources.

context_health.trim_strategy: Keep the goal, current_step, active constraints, latest tool output, and compressed episodic memory; drop stale scratchpads and old raw output.
context_health.stale_output_policy: Raw tool output expires after the subtask that generated it unless it is promoted into episodic memory or evidence.

self_check: Before responding, verify the output directly addresses the goal and the current_step done_condition.
